alkylation reagent: alkylation using 20 mM iodoacetamide with incubation in darkness for 45 min
alkylation concentration: alkylation using 20 mM iodoacetamide with incubation in darkness for 45 min
cleavage agent: Trypsin (Promega) was then added at a Trypsin:QBP mass ratio of 1:50
collision energy: stepped higher energy collision induced dissociation was applied with a normalized energy of 25, 35%
fragmentation method: stepped higher energy collision induced dissociation was applied
instrument: A Thermo Fisher Scientific Orbitrap Eclipse Tribrid mass spectrometer was used for this work
ionization type: nanoESI was performed by the application of a voltage to a SilicaTip emitter
reduction reagent: Samples were reduced with 8 mM DTT and incubated at 37°C for 45 min
reduction concentration: Samples were reduced with 8 mM DTT and incubated at 37°C for 45 min
